methodology.mockReason: Primary stress and push-to-failure concurrency use a local mock OpenAI-compatible embedding/LLM server. Live OpenAI was not used for failure ramps because API rate limits and quota errors would dominate long before SQLite or PostgreSQL contention, masking true AgentOrc/storage breaking points. A separate LIVE spot suite reports real-network latency for representative insert/search/ingest/compress paths.
methodology.failureCriteria: A concurrency level fails when errorRate > 1% OR p95 latency > 5s OR a hard integrity/exception failure occurs. Reports record lastHealthyLevel and breakingLevel.

sections.title: sqlite · Startup Benchmark
sections.description: Measures cold vs warm AgentOrc.ready() time (storage open, schema, embedding/LLM health probes).

details.definition: New AgentOrc + ready() against a freshly cleaned storage namespace each iteration
Reopen existing populated organization / DB file

sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Startup methodology

- Backend: **sqlite**
- **Cold**: cleaned storage + migrations + provider probes.
- **Warm**: reopen existing populated memory store.

sections.title: sqlite · Compression Benchmark
sections.description: Measures AgentOrc.compress() duration, active-set reduction, and storage delta.

content.text:
Compressed agent memory summary covering related operational notes. Key themes extracted from 2 messages: billing, meetings, deployments, and follow-ups. Source snippet: You are a memory compression engine for multi-agent systems.
Given related memories from a single agent, produce ONE concise summary that preserves:
- Key facts and decisions
- Imp

details.note: Percent is active memory-count reduction (archived rows retained for lineage). Disk reclaim requires VACUUM / dead-tuple cleanup.

content.text:
Compressed agent memory summary covering related operational notes. Key themes extracted from 2 messages: billing, meetings, deployments, and follow-ups. Source snippet: You are a memory compression engine for multi-agent systems.
Given related memories from a single agent, produce ONE concise summary that preserves:
- Key facts and decisions
- Imp

details.note: Percent is active memory-count reduction (archived rows retained for lineage). Disk reclaim requires VACUUM / dead-tuple cleanup.

sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Compression methodology

- Backend: **sqlite**
- Metric: **active-set reduction** = `(active_before − active_after) / active_before`.
- Archived sources remain in storage for history/lineage; `totalMemories` may rise by 1 (summary row).
- Live mode caps `limit` at 40 to control LLM cost.

sections.title: sqlite · Concurrency Baseline
sections.description: Fixed N concurrent AgentOrc.remember() writers on one client (AsyncMutex queueing).

details.note: Single AgentOrc instance; writes serialize via AsyncMutex.
Single AgentOrc instance; writes serialize via AsyncMutex.
Single AgentOrc instance; writes serialize via AsyncMutex.
Single AgentOrc instance; writes serialize via AsyncMutex.

sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Concurrency baseline methodology

- Backend: **sqlite** · levels 2, 4, 8, 16 × 15 writes.
- For push-to-failure ramps see Breaking Concurrency section.

sections.title: sqlite · Memory Usage Benchmark
sections.description: Heap/RSS snapshots before init, after inserts, after recall, and after close.
sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Memory methodology

- Backend: **sqlite** · process.memoryUsage() only (not DB RSS on remote Postgres).

sections.title: sqlite · Database Size Benchmark
sections.description: Measures storage growth after inserting 100 / 1k / 10k / 100k memories.
sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Database size methodology

- Backend: **sqlite**
- Size includes main `.db` plus `-wal` / `-shm` if present.

sections.title: sqlite · Search Benchmark
sections.description: Populates shared corpus, then benchmarks AgentOrc.recall() (avg / p95 / p99).
sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Search methodology

- Backend: **sqlite** · semantic recall topK=5

sections.title: sqlite · Retrieval Benchmark
sections.description: Benchmarks top-5 / top-10 / top-20 recall against shared corpora.
sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Retrieval methodology

- Backend: **sqlite**

sections.title: sqlite · Insert Benchmark
sections.description: Measures AgentOrc.remember() throughput and latency (embed → persist → index).

details.label: insert-100
details.note: embed vs store split from AGENTORC_PROFILE samples (n≤20)

details.label: insert-1000
details.note: embed vs store split from AGENTORC_PROFILE samples (n≤20)

sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Insert methodology

- Backend: **sqlite**
- Stage timings: embedding generation vs storage write (mutex + SQL + index).
- Identical mock embeddings and datasets across backends.

sections.title: sqlite · Hybrid Search Benchmark
sections.description: Compares semantic-only vs hybrid BM25 fusion latency on shared corpora.
sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Hybrid methodology

- Backend: **sqlite** · BM25 keyword index warm from shared corpus.

sections.title: sqlite · Metadata Filter Benchmark
sections.description: Recall latency with agent filters and meta.eq / and / or / gte selectivity.
sections.extraMarkdown:
#### Filter methodology

- Backend: **sqlite** · corpus 1000

sections.title: sqlite · MMR / Rerank Benchmark
sections.description: Latency impact of MMR on/off. Rerank flag exercises graceful no-op without a configured reranker in mock mode.
sections.extraMarkdown:
#### MMR / rerank methodology

- Backend: **sqlite**
- Mock mode has no reranker provider — `rerank: true` measures graceful skip overhead only.

sections.title: sqlite · Ingest Benchmark
sections.description: Ingest throughput for markdown/text/json/PDF/DOCX fixtures (when present).
